steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what can “most reasonably be concluded” about krill consumption between sei and northern right whales. The passage states baleen whales eat up to 30% of their mass per day, gives the sei whale a max mass of 25,000 kg, and the northern right whale a max mass of 80,000 kg consuming “as much as 24,000 kg of krill per day.”

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘The northern right whale is able to eat more krill per day than the sei whale is.’: At 30% of body mass, the sei whale can eat up to 7,500 kg/day; the northern right whale eats up to 24,000 kg/day. The conclusion is directly supported. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Both the sei whale and the northern right whale can eat as much as 24,000 kg of krill per day.’: The 24,000 kg figure applies only to the northern right whale; the sei whale's smaller mass makes this impossible. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘The quantities of krill consumed by northern right whales have made it difficult for sei whales to find sufficient food.’: Food scarcity between the species is never mentioned. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Most baleen whales include krill in their diets, but the sei whale is less likely than the northern right whale to do so.’: The passage says “like all species of baleen whales, the sei whale feeds on…krill” — both eat krill. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
